title: NOC 72400 – Construction millwrights and industrial mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Construction Millwrights and Industrial Mechanics (NOC 72400) are the precision masters of Canada’s industrial engine. Often referred to as "Industrial MacGyvers," these professionals are responsible for installing, maintaining, and overhauling the stationary machinery that powers everything from nuclear plants and automotive factories to massive food processing centers. Operating at the TEER 2 level, the role has shifted in 2026 toward high-tech diagnostic integration, where millwrights use laser alignment tools, thermal imaging, and vibration analysis to maintain increasingly complex robotic and automated systems.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires a college diploma, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Technical Interpretation: Reading blueprints, diagrams, and schematic drawings to determine the layout and work procedures for complex machinery.
Precision Installation: Installing, aligning, and moving stationary industrial machinery (pumps, fans, conveyors, generators) using laser alignment and power tools.
Malfunction Detection: Inspecting and examining machinery to investigate irregularities and malfunctions using vibration analysis and thermal imaging.
System Maintenance: Installing and maintaining power transmission, vacuum, hydraulic, and pneumatic systems, as well as PLCs.
Fabrication: Operating machine tools such as lathes and grinders to fabricate specialized parts required during machinery overhaul or set-up.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over five years of work experience and industry courses is required for certification.

Certification: Trade certification for industrial mechanics (millwrights) is available but voluntary in all provinces and territories. However, it is highly recommended for higher pay and union roles.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying industrial contracts.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, with a projected moderate risk of labour shortage through 2033 due to a wave of senior-level retirements:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for "Skilled Trades" including NOC 72400. Minimum scores for regional draws in Southwestern Ontario started as low as 53, reflecting the urgent need for millwrights in the province’s manufacturing and energy sectors (including nuclear refurbishments).

British Columbia: High Priority. NOC 72400 is currently on the BC PNP Priority Occupations List for construction. Candidates with a valid trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC and a job offer benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.

Alberta: Strongest Demand. Alberta reports significant hiring difficulties, with 53% of employers experiencing challenges. Median wages are among the highest at $38.86/hr, with top earners in industrial settings reaching $68.00/hr.

National Trend: Over 95% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. Roughly 36% of the current workforce is aged 50 or older, meaning thousands of leadership positions will open up as they retire over the next few years.
Why Work in Canada as a Millwright?
Lead Industrial Modernization: Gain hands-on experience with 2026-grade technologies, including AI-driven predictive maintenance and Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs).
Excellent Compensation & Stability: As a TEER 2 trade, your median hourly wage in Canada is $40.00/hr, and 68% of millwrights work all year round—higher than the national average.
Cross-Sector Versatility: Your skills are in demand across manufacturing (51%), mining, oil and gas (10%), and utilities.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "Construction" and "Skilled Trades" draws, offering a reliable path to Permanent Residency.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72400 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern Ontario) under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.
BC PNP Construction Stream: Provides priority processing and targeted invitations for millwrights with a valid trade certificate and a job offer in B.C.
2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Millwrights with 12 months of full-time experience (updated 2026 requirement) are eligible for specialized trades draws with lower CRS cutoffs.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Millwrights are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
